title: cocculus hirsutus
intro: Cocculus hirsutus, also known as the Katra or Kattha plant, is a climbing vine native to the tropical regions of India and Southeast Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for centuries due to its medicinal properties, including its anthelmintic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Cocculus hirsutus include its ability to treat digestive issues, such as constipation and diarrhea, as well as its potential to lower blood sugar levels and reduce stress. Cocculus hirsutus can be prepared into various herbal forms, including powder, capsules, and decoctions, which are often used in Ayurvedic and Unani medicine to promote overall well-being.
